relevant_files.rationale: Core scene-side logic (696 lines): handles add/delete/move components, auto-mount orchestration, cross-scope mount dialog, node UUID lookup. Contains getNodeByUuid with O(n) recursive traversal on every call - major performance bottleneck. Also has performAutoMount which creates new PropertyMounter instance each invocation.

Panel UI logic (1972 lines): handles search input, component list rendering, settings page, drag-to-reorder, keyboard navigation, theme switching, context menus. Monolithic file mixing UI rendering, event handling, state management, and IPC communication. Primary candidate for modularization.

Data persistence layer (621 lines): manages favorites, usage stats, settings (auto-mount, shortcuts, themes, confirmations). Uses synchronous fs.writeFileSync on every single operation (forceSaveToFile called after each change). validateDataConsistency reads file back immediately after writing - redundant I/O. saveData has logical bug with || vs ?? for boolean defaults.

Auto property mounting engine (700 lines): two-phase nearest-first search algorithm, node tree traversal, name matching with flexible letter matching. Well-structured but debugComponentStructure has hardcoded test property names ('labelOutdsa', 'dsdsadsa'). Instance-level mountResults state creates potential cross-call contamination.

Component search with scoring (234 lines): exact/prefix/contains/fuzzy matching with favorite weight bonus. Clean module but escapeRegExp is called but regex is never actually used (input is compared with string methods). Search result limit hardcoded to 20.

Panel styling (1433 lines): extensive CSS with 6 theme variants (vibrant-dark, warm-dark, cyberpunk, forest, sunset, ocean). CSS variables via data-theme attributes. Large file size suggests potential for CSS-in-JS or preprocessor optimization.

Build script (234 lines): assembles styles.css + template.html + logic.js into panel/index.js. Simple concatenation build system - works but no watch mode, no minification, no source maps. Manual rebuild required after every change.
Panel HTML template (37 lines): minimal structure with main page and settings page. Settings content generated dynamically in logic.js renderSettingsContent(). SVG icons inlined directly.
Generated output file (3445 lines): assembled by build.js from styles.css + template.html + logic.js. Should never be edited directly. Registered as panel entry in package.json.

Logging module (108 lines): two-level logging (ALL/ERROR_ONLY). Uses Editor.log for all output levels including errors - should use Editor.warn/Editor.error for proper severity routing. Silent catch in _safeLog calls Editor.error which could itself throw.

Internationalization helper (167 lines): supports zh/en with template parameter replacement. Only zh translation file exists - en falls back to zh. detectSystemLanguage checks Editor.lang and environment variables.
Plugin entry point (21 lines): minimal load/unload hooks and open/close messages. No initialization logic - all work delegated to panel and scene-accessor.

Auto-dock script (73 lines): hacks into Cocos Creator internal renderer to dock plugin panel next to Inspector. Uses setInterval polling and monkey-patches panelMgr.close and dropMgr.items. Fragile and version-dependent.

Only test file (459 lines): covers PropertyMounter matchNodeName, traverseNodeTree, findBestMatchInSubtree, autoMountProperties. Uses custom Cocos Creator engine mock environment. No tests for search-engine, data-manager, scene-accessor, or panel logic.
Design token definitions for the 6 theme variants. Contains color palettes, spacing, typography values used by styles.css.
Runtime user data file: stores favorites, usage counts, and settings. Written synchronously by data-manager.js. Committed to git which means user-specific data pollutes the repository.

existing_patterns.name: Singleton Module Pattern
existing_patterns.description: Core modules (data-manager, search-engine, logger) export singleton instances via `module.exports = new ClassName()`. Ensures single shared state across the plugin.
existing_patterns.strength: Consistent pattern across all service modules
existing_patterns.weakness: Makes unit testing difficult - no way to create fresh instances or inject mocks

existing_patterns.name: Scene Script IPC Bridge
existing_patterns.description: scene-accessor.js runs in Cocos Creator's scene process. Panel communicates via Editor.Ipc.sendToScene/sendToPanel message passing. Functions are keyed by string names like 'add-component', 'input-query'.
existing_patterns.weakness: No type safety on IPC messages, string-based routing is fragile, no request/response correlation

existing_patterns.name: Build-Time Panel Assembly
existing_patterns.description: Panel source split into styles.css, template.html, logic.js. build.js concatenates them into index.js using Editor.Panel.extend() format required by Cocos Creator.
existing_patterns.strength: Separation of concerns for development, single file for runtime
existing_patterns.weakness: Manual build step, no watch mode, no hot reload

existing_patterns.name: Two-Phase Nearest-First Search
existing_patterns.description: Property auto-mount uses Phase 1 (search current node subtree) then Phase 2 (climb ancestor subtrees) with shared visited set. Phase 2 results require user confirmation via dialog.
existing_patterns.strength: Intelligent proximity-based matching, prevents accidental cross-scope mounts
existing_patterns.weakness: Dialog is synchronous and blocking, no batch confirmation UI

existing_patterns.name: Synchronous File Persistence
existing_patterns.description: DataManager uses fs.writeFileSync for every data change (favorites, usage, settings). validateDataConsistency re-reads file immediately after write.
existing_patterns.strength: Data integrity - no lost writes on crash
existing_patterns.weakness: Blocks the process on every change, redundant I/O from validate-after-save pattern

existing_patterns.name: Theme System via CSS Data Attributes
existing_patterns.description: 6 theme variants defined via [data-theme='xxx'] CSS selectors with CSS custom properties. Theme applied by setting data-theme attribute on container element.
existing_patterns.strength: Clean theme switching without JS manipulation of individual styles
existing_patterns.weakness: 1433 lines of CSS with lots of duplication across themes

architecture_constraints.impact: Must use Editor.Panel.extend(), Editor.Ipc for communication, scene-script for scene access. No modern module bundlers - plain CommonJS require(). No ES modules, no async/await in scene scripts.
scene-accessor.js runs in a separate V8 context with access to cc.* runtime. Panel runs in Electron renderer. All communication must go through IPC. Cannot share object references.
No webpack/rollup/vite. Panel assembly done by simple build.js concatenation. Cannot use TypeScript, JSX, CSS preprocessors, or modern JS features that require transpilation.
Editor.Dialog.messageBox is synchronous, blocks the scene process. Cross-scope mount confirmation freezes the editor until user responds.
Only one panel instance allowed. All state (curMatchs, hoveredComponentButton, etc.) lives on the panel object. No state management pattern.

integration_points.description: Global registry of all component classes in the current Cocos Creator project. Used by search-engine.searchComponents() as the component database. Read-only access.
Returns array of currently selected node UUIDs in the scene hierarchy. Used to determine which nodes receive added components. Called frequently in scene-accessor.
Primary communication channel between panel (renderer) and scene-accessor (scene process). All component operations go through this bridge.
Cocos Creator internal API to read component property definitions including type constructors. Used by PropertyMounter.parseComponentProperties() to discover mountable properties.
Synchronous modal dialog for cross-scope mount confirmation. Blocking call in scene process.
Core Cocos Creator APIs for adding and removing components. Used in scene-accessor add-component and del-component handlers.
Marks the current scene as modified so Cocos Creator shows the unsaved indicator. Called after successful component add/delete/move/mount operations.

tech_debt.title: getNodeByUuid O(n) recursive traversal
tech_debt.description: scene-accessor.js getNodeByUuid() performs full DFS from scene root on every call. Called multiple times per operation (add-component iterates selected nodes, each calling getNodeByUuid). No caching. For scenes with thousands of nodes, this creates significant latency.

tech_debt.title: Synchronous file I/O on every data change
tech_debt.description: DataManager.forceSaveToFile() uses fs.writeFileSync and is called after every single operation (add favorite, record usage, change setting). validateDataConsistency() reads the file back right after writing. This blocks the Node.js event loop unnecessarily.

tech_debt.title: saveData boolean defaults use || instead of ??
tech_debt.description: In data-manager.js saveData(), boolean settings like `enabled: data?.settings?.autoPropertyMount?.enabled || true` will always be true because `false || true === true`. Should use nullish coalescing (??) for boolean fields.

tech_debt.title: Monolithic panel/logic.js (1972 lines)
tech_debt.description: Single file contains all panel logic: search input handling, component list rendering, drag-and-drop, keyboard navigation, settings page, theme switching, context menus, IPC communication. Extremely difficult to maintain, test, or extend.

tech_debt.title: Hardcoded debug property names in PropertyMounter
tech_debt.description: debugComponentStructure() has hardcoded test property names ['label', 'labelOutdsa', 'dsdsadsa'] that appear to be development leftovers.

tech_debt.title: user-data.json committed to git
tech_debt.description: User-specific data file (favorites, usage stats, custom settings) is tracked in git. Should be in .gitignore to prevent user data from polluting the repository.

tech_debt.title: No test coverage for search-engine, data-manager, scene-accessor, panel
tech_debt.description: Only property-mounter.js has tests. SearchEngine scoring logic, DataManager persistence, and scene-accessor component operations have zero test coverage.

tech_debt.title: PropertyMounter instance-level mountResults state
tech_debt.description: PropertyMounter stores mountResults as instance state (this.mountResults). If an instance is reused across calls without reset, results accumulate incorrectly. scene-accessor creates new instance each call (mitigating), but the design is fragile.

tech_debt.title: escapeRegExp called but regex never used
tech_debt.description: SearchEngine.searchComponents() calls escapeRegExp on input but then uses string comparison methods (indexOf, startsWith) not regex. The escaping is unnecessary overhead.

tech_debt.title: Logger uses Editor.log for all severity levels
tech_debt.description: Logger._safeLog() routes all messages through Editor.log regardless of level. Should use Editor.warn for warnings and Editor.error for errors to enable proper filtering in Cocos Creator console.

tech_debt.title: Duplicate getStats method in DataManager
tech_debt.description: DataManager has two getStats() methods (lines 224-238 and 436-442). The second one silently overrides the first. They return slightly different structures.

tech_debt.title: template-auto-dock.js monkey-patches internal APIs
tech_debt.description: Auto-dock script patches panelMgr.close and dropMgr.items by wrapping original functions. Relies on internal file paths (dock-utils.js, drag-drop.js, renderer/panel.js) that may change across Cocos Creator versions.

optimization_opportunities.title: Cache node UUID lookups
optimization_opportunities.description: Add a WeakMap-based UUID-to-node cache in scene-accessor that invalidates on scene change. Eliminates repeated O(n) tree traversals. Could reduce latency by 10-100x for multi-node operations.

optimization_opportunities.title: Debounce/batch data persistence
optimization_opportunities.description: Replace synchronous write-on-every-change with debounced async writes. Accumulate changes in memory and flush to disk after a configurable delay (e.g., 500ms). Remove redundant validateDataConsistency after save.

optimization_opportunities.title: Add search-engine tests
optimization_opportunities.description: SearchEngine scoring logic (exact/prefix/contains/fuzzy matching, favorite weight bonus, sort stability) is complex but has zero tests. Add comprehensive tests covering edge cases in scoring and ranking.

optimization_opportunities.title: Modularize panel/logic.js
optimization_opportunities.description: Split 1972-line monolith into focused modules: search-controller.js, component-list-renderer.js, settings-controller.js, keyboard-navigation.js, drag-handler.js. Build.js would need to handle multiple source files.

optimization_opportunities.title: Component grouping/categorization in search results
optimization_opportunities.description: Currently all components are shown in a flat list. Adding categories (Built-in, Custom, Third-party) or tags would help users find components faster in large projects.

optimization_opportunities.title: Batch auto-mount with visual preview
optimization_opportunities.description: Instead of mounting one component at a time with synchronous dialogs, provide a batch preview UI showing all proposed mounts (Phase 1 + Phase 2) with checkboxes. User confirms once for all.

optimization_opportunities.title: Add pinyin search support for Chinese component names
optimization_opportunities.description: Chinese developers may name components in Chinese. Adding pinyin matching to search-engine would enable typing 'anniu' to find components named with Chinese characters for button.

optimization_opportunities.title: Component usage analytics dashboard
optimization_opportunities.description: Usage data is collected but only shown as simple counts. A mini dashboard showing most-used components, usage trends, and suggestions based on project patterns would add value.

optimization_opportunities.title: Undo/redo support for component operations
optimization_opportunities.description: Currently no undo support for add/delete/move component operations. Integrating with Cocos Creator's undo system or implementing a simple command pattern would prevent accidental data loss.

optimization_opportunities.title: Component templates / presets
optimization_opportunities.description: Allow users to save frequently-used component combinations as templates (e.g., 'Button = cc.Sprite + cc.Button + cc.Widget'). One-click application of component sets to selected nodes.

optimization_opportunities.title: Fix boolean default bug in saveData
optimization_opportunities.description: Replace `||` with `??` for boolean settings in DataManager.saveData() to allow users to actually disable features (currently false values are overridden to true).

optimization_opportunities.title: Watch mode for build.js
optimization_opportunities.description: Add file watcher to build.js that auto-rebuilds panel/index.js when styles.css, template.html, or logic.js change. Would significantly improve development experience.

optimization_opportunities.title: Component dependency visualization
optimization_opportunities.description: Show which components depend on other components (e.g., cc.Button requires cc.Sprite). Warn when deleting a component that others depend on. Could leverage property-mounter's property analysis.

optimization_opportunities.title: Smart property mount preview in panel
optimization_opportunities.description: Before executing auto-mount, show a preview in the panel UI listing which properties would be mounted to which nodes, with match scores and types. Users can review and toggle individual mounts.

optimization_opportunities.title: Proper logger severity routing
optimization_opportunities.description: Route logger.warn to Editor.warn, logger.error to Editor.error instead of all going through Editor.log. Enables proper filtering in Cocos Creator console and avoids the recursive error in _safeLog catch block.

inspiration_sources.relevance: Unity's fuzzy search panel supports type-ahead, result categorization (assets, components, menu items), and search providers. The provider pattern could inspire a pluggable search architecture for this tool.
The command palette's MRU (most recently used) sorting and inline keyboard navigation patterns could improve the component search UX. The ':' prefix for filtering categories is also relevant.
The concept of component presets/templates is similar to IDE snippet systems. Users define named templates with variable placeholders, expanding them with a shortcut.
CC 3.x uses TypeScript decorators (@property) for component property definitions. Understanding the migration path could help plan forward-compatible features, especially for auto-mount.

Their action-based search model (type to search, then apply actions like add/favorite/inspect) maps well to the component search workflow. The visual feedback patterns for actions are also applicable.

_metadata.test_coverage_summary: Only property-mounter.js has tests (459 lines covering matchNodeName, traverseNodeTree, findBestMatchInSubtree, autoMountProperties). Zero coverage for search-engine, data-manager, scene-accessor, panel logic.
_metadata.architecture_summary: Cocos Creator 2.4.x plugin with scene-script (scene-accessor.js) + panel (logic.js) IPC architecture. Plain CommonJS, no build toolchain beyond simple concatenation. 6 theme variants, i18n support (zh/en).
